questions.q: The Kakatiya dynasty had its capital at which city?
questions.explanation: Warangal (Orugallu) in present-day Telangana was the principal capital of the Kakatiyas.

questions.q: The Kakatiya kingdom was centred in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Kakatiya heartland lay in present-day Telangana, around Warangal.

questions.q: The Kakatiyas rose to prominence chiefly between which centuries CE?
questions.explanation: The Kakatiyas were a major power roughly from the 12th to the 14th century CE.

questions.q: The Kakatiya realm finally fell to which power in the early 14th century?
questions.explanation: The Kakatiyas were overcome by the forces of the Delhi Sultanate in the early 14th century.

questions.q: The Kakatiyas are especially remembered for building what type of water-management structures?
questions.explanation: The Kakatiyas built numerous large irrigation tanks to support agriculture in the Deccan.

questions.q: The famous Thousand Pillar Temple associated with the Kakatiyas stands at which place?
questions.explanation: The Thousand Pillar Temple at Hanamkonda near Warangal is a celebrated Kakatiya monument.

questions.q: The principal language patronised by the Kakatiyas, still spoken in their old heartland, is?
questions.explanation: The Kakatiyas patronised the Telugu language and helped shape Telugu cultural identity.

questions.q: Warangal Fort, a major Kakatiya site, is best known for what distinctive feature?
questions.explanation: Warangal Fort is famed for its carved stone gateways, the Kakatiya Kala Thoranam.

questions.q: Which legendary queen ruled the Kakatiya kingdom in her own right?
questions.explanation: Rudrama Devi was a Kakatiya ruler and one of the few women to rule a major Indian kingdom.

questions.q: The Kakatiya dynasty ruled mainly over which broad region of India?
questions.explanation: The Kakatiyas were a Deccan power based in the Telugu-speaking interior of the peninsula.

questions.q: Which Kakatiya ruler is generally regarded as the dynasty's most powerful king and father of Rudrama Devi?
questions.explanation: Ganapati Deva had the longest reign and the widest expansion; Rudrama Devi was his daughter and successor.

questions.q: Under which last Kakatiya ruler did the kingdom fall to the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Prataparudra II was the last Kakatiya sovereign, captured during the Delhi Sultanate conquest around 1323 CE.

questions.q: Before asserting full independence, the Kakatiyas were originally feudatories of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Kakatiyas began as subordinates of the Western Chalukyas of Kalyani before becoming independent.

questions.q: Which large irrigation tank is famously attributed to a Kakatiya minister and remains in use today?
questions.explanation: The Pakhal tank, built under Ganapati Deva's reign, exemplifies Kakatiya water engineering.

questions.q: The Kakatiya temples are noted for a distinctive construction technique using what material for parts of the structure?
questions.explanation: Kakatiya builders used unusually light bricks for upper temple towers, a hallmark of their architecture.

questions.q: Which European traveller praised the Kakatiya queen Rudrama Devi's just rule?
questions.explanation: The Venetian traveller Marco Polo recorded admiration for the queen's governance of the realm.

questions.q: The Ramappa Temple, a Kakatiya masterpiece inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: The Ramappa (Rudreshwara) Temple is a Shiva temple, named after its sculptor and recognised by UNESCO in 2021.

questions.q: The Kakatiyas are credited with developing a class of warrior chiefs who held land in return for military service, called?
questions.explanation: The Kakatiya nayaka system tied warrior chiefs to the state through land-for-service arrangements.

questions.q: Which Delhi Sultanate commander led the campaign that captured Warangal and Prataparudra?
options: Ulugh Khan (the future Muhammad bin Tughlaq)
questions.explanation: Ulugh Khan, son of Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq and later Sultan Muhammad bin Tughlaq, took Warangal around 1323 CE.

questions.q: The name 'Kakatiya' is traditionally linked to the worship of which goddess?
questions.explanation: The dynasty's name is associated with the goddess Kakati, after whom the family is said to be named.

questions.q: Warangal Fort was notable for being defended by how many concentric walls in its design?
questions.explanation: Warangal's fortifications featured a series of three concentric defensive walls around the citadel.

questions.q: After the fall of the Kakatiyas, much of their old territory and traditions were inherited by which later empire?
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara, founded soon after, drew on Kakatiya nayaka traditions and absorbed parts of their lands.

questions.q: Which Kakatiya king is credited with making Warangal (Orugallu) the dynasty's capital and strengthening it?
questions.explanation: Ganapati Deva developed Orugallu/Warangal as a fortified capital during his long 13th-century reign.

questions.q: The Ramappa Temple was completed in which century under Kakatiya patronage?
questions.explanation: An inscription dates the Ramappa Temple to the 13th century CE, built under a general of Ganapati Deva.

questions.q: Which Kakatiya ruler is sometimes counted as an early king who threw off Chalukya overlordship in the mid-12th century?
questions.explanation: Prola II is credited with asserting Kakatiya independence from the Western Chalukyas in the mid-12th century.

questions.q: The general who built the Ramappa Temple served under which Kakatiya monarch?
questions.explanation: Recharla Rudra, a general of Ganapati Deva, is credited with commissioning the Ramappa Temple.

questions.q: The captured Prataparudra II reportedly died in what manner while being taken to Delhi?
options: He was crowned a vassal sultan
He died en route to Delhi as a prisoner
questions.explanation: Tradition holds Prataparudra II died on the way to Delhi after his capture, ending Kakatiya rule.

questions.q: The Kakatiyas were notable in medieval India for relatively open social recruitment into which group?
questions.explanation: Historians note the Kakatiya nayaka class drew men from varied social backgrounds, an open recruitment for the era.

questions.q: Approximately when did the Delhi Sultanate finally annex the Kakatiya kingdom?
questions.explanation: The decisive conquest of Warangal by Tughlaq forces occurred around 1323 CE.

questions.q: The famed carved gateways of Warangal Fort have been adopted as an official emblem of which present-day state?
questions.explanation: The Kakatiya Kala Thoranam (Warangal gateway) features on the official emblem of Telangana state.
